description: Standalone filesystem driver packages implementing VirtualFileSystem from @secure-exec/core, plus a shared conformance test suite and Docker test helpers exported from core.

userStories.title: Add shared FS conformance test suite to core
userStories.description: As a driver author, I need a reusable test suite so every VFS implementation is validated against the same contract.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/core/src/test/file-system.ts exports defineFsDriverTests(config) function
FsDriverTestConfig interface includes name, createFs, cleanup, and capabilities fields

Core tests always run: writeFile+readFile round-trip (string and binary), readTextFile, readFile ENOENT, writeFile auto-creates parents, exists for files/dirs/missing, stat for files/dirs/missing, removeFile, readDir (children only, no . or ..), readDirWithTypes, rename, realpath, overwrite
Conditional tests gated on capabilities: symlinks, hardLinks, permissions, utimes, truncate, pread, mkdir, removeDir
Sub-path export ./test/file-system added to packages/core/package.json exports
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Docker test helpers to core
userStories.description: As a driver test author, I need helpers to spin up Docker containers (MinIO, Sandbox Agent) for integration tests.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/core/src/test/docker.ts exports startContainer, startMinioContainer, and startSandboxAgentContainer
startContainer uses child_process.execFile to run docker CLI, supports port mappings, env, health checks
startMinioContainer starts MinIO, waits for health, creates test bucket via docker exec mc mb
startSandboxAgentContainer starts sandbox-agent container and returns connected SandboxAgent client
Sub-path export ./test/docker added to packages/core/package.json exports
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Rewrite host-dir and overlay backend tests to use shared suite
userStories.description: As a developer, I want existing core backend tests to use the shared suite so we don't maintain duplicate test logic.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/core/tests/host-dir-backend.test.ts imports defineFsDriverTests and runs with correct capabilities (symlinks: false, hardLinks: false, permissions: true, utimes: true, truncate: true, pread: true)
packages/core/tests/overlay-backend.test.ts imports defineFsDriverTests and runs with correct capabilities (all true)
Both test files pass when run with vitest
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Scaffold fs-s3 package
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the fs-s3 package scaffolded with package.json, tsconfig, and source structure.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/fs-s3/package.json exists with name @rivet-dev/agent-os-fs-s3, ES module config, vitest test runner
packages/fs-s3/tsconfig.json extends root config
packages/fs-s3/src/index.ts exports createS3Backend function and S3FsOptions type
Dependencies: @aws-sdk/client-s3, @secure-exec/core
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement S3 backend: read/write/exists/stat
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the core S3 filesystem operations so files can be stored and retrieved from S3.
acceptanceCriteria: createS3Backend returns a VirtualFileSystem
writeFile puts object to S3 with correct key prefix
readFile gets object from S3
readTextFile decodes to string
exists checks HeadObject, falls back to ListObjectsV2 for directory detection
stat returns size and isDirectory correctly
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement S3 backend: directory listing, rename, pread, no-ops, ENOSYS
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the remaining S3 operations for full VFS compliance.
acceptanceCriteria: readDir and readDirWithTypes list objects under prefix with single-level depth
rename uses CopyObject + DeleteObject
pread uses S3 range requests
mkdir/createDir/removeDir are no-ops
symlink/link/readlink/chmod/chown throw ENOSYS
utimes is a no-op
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add S3 backend tests using shared suite and MinIO
userStories.description: As a developer, I need integration tests for the S3 backend running against MinIO.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/fs-s3/tests/s3.test.ts imports defineFsDriverTests and startMinioContainer
beforeAll starts MinIO container with 60s timeout
afterAll stops container
Capabilities: symlinks false, hardLinks false, permissions false, utimes false, truncate true, pread true, mkdir false, removeDir false
Tests pass against MinIO (requires Docker)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Scaffold fs-sandbox package
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the fs-sandbox package scaffolded with package.json, tsconfig, and source structure.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/fs-sandbox/package.json exists with name @rivet-dev/agent-os-fs-sandbox, ES module config, vitest test runner
packages/fs-sandbox/tsconfig.json extends root config
packages/fs-sandbox/src/index.ts exports createSandboxBackend function and SandboxFsOptions type
Dependencies: sandbox-agent, @secure-exec/core
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement sandbox backend
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the sandbox filesystem backend that delegates all operations to the Sandbox Agent SDK.
acceptanceCriteria: createSandboxBackend returns a VirtualFileSystem
All VFS methods map to correct SDK methods per the spec mapping table
readFile/writeFile/readDir/readDirWithTypes/mkdir/exists/stat/removeFile/removeDir/rename all delegate correctly
symlink/readlink/link/chmod/chown/utimes throw ENOSYS
truncate reads, slices, writes back
pread reads full file and slices
lstat delegates to stat
realpath returns normalized path
VirtualStat mapping matches spec (mode, size, isDirectory, timestamps from modified field)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add sandbox backend tests using shared suite
userStories.description: As a developer, I need integration tests for the sandbox backend running against a Sandbox Agent container.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/fs-sandbox/tests/sandbox.test.ts imports defineFsDriverTests and startSandboxAgentContainer
beforeAll starts sandbox agent container with 120s timeout
afterAll stops container
Capabilities: symlinks false, hardLinks false, permissions false, utimes false, truncate false, pread false, mkdir true, removeDir true
Tests pass against Sandbox Agent (requires Docker)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Scaffold fs-sqlite package
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the fs-sqlite package scaffolded with package.json, tsconfig, and source structure.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/fs-sqlite/package.json exists with name @rivet-dev/agent-os-fs-sqlite, ES module config, vitest test runner
packages/fs-sqlite/tsconfig.json extends root config
packages/fs-sqlite/src/index.ts exports createSqliteBackend function and SqliteFsOptions type
Dependencies: better-sqlite3, @secure-exec/core
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement SQLite backend: schema and core read/write
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the SQLite schema and basic file read/write operations.
acceptanceCriteria: createSqliteBackend opens SQLite db at dbPath and creates fs_entries table with correct schema
Schema has path, entry_type, content, mode, uid, gid, size, nlink, symlink_target, and timestamp columns
writeFile inserts/replaces file entries, auto-creates parent directories
readFile returns content blob
readTextFile decodes to string
exists checks for path in table
stat returns correct VirtualStat from row data
removeFile deletes file entry
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement SQLite backend: directories, rename, symlinks, permissions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the remaining SQLite operations for full VFS compliance.
acceptanceCriteria: mkdir/createDir creates directory entries
readDir/readDirWithTypes queries by path prefix with single-level depth filter
removeDir removes empty directories, throws ENOTEMPTY for non-empty
rename is transactional UPDATE on path (and all children for directories)
symlink creates entry with entry_type='symlink' and symlink_target
readlink returns symlink_target
lstat returns symlink entry directly
stat/readFile follow symlink chains with loop detection
link creates hard link (nlink incremented)
chmod/chown update mode/uid/gid
utimes updates atime/mtime
truncate slices content blob
pread reads content slice at offset
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add SQLite backend tests using shared suite
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests for the SQLite backend using in-memory databases.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/fs-sqlite/tests/sqlite.test.ts imports defineFsDriverTests
Uses createSqliteBackend({ dbPath: ':memory:' })
Capabilities: all true (symlinks, hardLinks, permissions, utimes, truncate, pread)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Remove S3 backend from core and update imports
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the old S3 backend deleted from core so the new fs-s3 package is the single source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/core/src/backends/s3-backend.ts is deleted
S3BackendOptions and createS3Backend removed from packages/core/src/index.ts exports
MountConfigS3 handling in agent-os.ts updated to use @rivet-dev/agent-os-fs-s3
@rivet-dev/agent-os-fs-s3 added as dependency of core
@aws-sdk/client-s3 removed from core's direct dependencies
packages/core/tests/s3-backend.test.ts is deleted
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add startPostgresContainer Docker test helper
userStories.description: As a driver test author, I need a helper to spin up a Postgres container for integration tests, following the same pattern as startMinioContainer.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/core/src/test/docker.ts exports startPostgresContainer(options?)
Options accept optional user, password, database, and image tag
Starts postgres Docker container, waits for health check (pg_isready or SELECT 1 via pg client)
Returns { container, connectionString, host, port, user, password, database }
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Scaffold fs-postgres package
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the fs-postgres package scaffolded with package.json, tsconfig, and source structure.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/fs-postgres/package.json exists with name @rivet-dev/agent-os-fs-postgres, ES module config, vitest test runner
packages/fs-postgres/tsconfig.json extends root config
packages/fs-postgres/src/index.ts exports createPostgresBackend function and PostgresFsOptions type
Dependencies: pg (or postgres), @secure-exec/core
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Postgres backend: schema and core read/write
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Postgres schema and basic file read/write operations.
acceptanceCriteria: createPostgresBackend accepts PostgresFsOptions with connectionString or pool, optional prefix/schema

Auto-creates fs_entries table on init with: path TEXT PRIMARY KEY, entry_type TEXT (file/directory/symlink), content BYTEA, mode INTEGER, uid INTEGER, gid INTEGER, size BIGINT, nlink INTEGER, symlink_target TEXT, atime_ms DOUBLE PRECISION, mtime_ms DOUBLE PRECISION, ctime_ms DOUBLE PRECISION, birthtime_ms DOUBLE PRECISION
writeFile inserts/upserts file entries, auto-creates parent directories
readFile returns content as Uint8Array
readTextFile decodes to string
exists checks for path in table
stat returns correct VirtualStat from row data
removeFile deletes file entry
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Postgres backend: directories, rename, symlinks, permissions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the remaining Postgres operations for full VFS compliance.
acceptanceCriteria: mkdir/createDir creates directory entries
readDir/readDirWithTypes queries by path prefix with single-level depth filter
removeDir removes empty directories, throws ENOTEMPTY for non-empty
rename is transactional UPDATE on path (and all children for directories)
symlink creates entry with entry_type='symlink' and symlink_target
readlink returns symlink_target
lstat returns symlink entry directly
stat/readFile follow symlink chains with loop detection
link creates hard link (nlink incremented)
chmod/chown update mode/uid/gid
utimes updates atime/mtime
truncate slices content via substring/overlay
pread reads content slice at offset via substring
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Postgres backend tests using shared suite and Docker Postgres
userStories.description: As a developer, I need integration tests for the Postgres backend running against a real Postgres container.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/fs-postgres/tests/postgres.test.ts imports defineFsDriverTests and startPostgresContainer
beforeAll starts Postgres container with 60s timeout
afterAll stops container
Each test run uses createPostgresBackend with unique schema or prefix for isolation
Capabilities: all true (symlinks, hardLinks, permissions, utimes, truncate, pread)
Tests pass against real Postgres (requires Docker)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Harden shared test suite with error code assertions and missing edge cases
userStories.description: As a driver author, I need the shared test suite to assert specific error codes and cover missing edge cases so bugs cannot hide behind vague assertions.
acceptanceCriteria: All .rejects.toThrow() assertions verify the specific KernelErrorCode (ENOENT, ENOTEMPTY, EISDIR, ENOSYS, etc.), not just that an error is thrown
Add test: readFile on a directory path throws EISDIR
Add test: removeFile on missing file throws ENOENT
Add test: readDir on missing directory throws ENOENT
Add test: readTextFile on missing file throws ENOENT
Add test: removeDir on non-empty directory throws ENOTEMPTY (gated on removeDir capability)
Add test: mkdir without recursive flag on nested path throws ENOENT when parent is missing (gated on mkdir capability)
Add test: rename of missing source throws ENOENT
Add test: rename across directories works (e.g. /a/old.txt to /b/new.txt)
Add test: rename a directory with children moves all children
Add test: createDir creates a single-level directory (gated on mkdir capability)
Add test: writeFile with empty string produces zero-length file, readFile returns empty
Add test: writeFile with empty Uint8Array produces zero-length file
Add test: stat.size is exactly the byte length written (strict equality, not >=)
Add test: realpath normalizes paths like /a/../b to /b
Use describe.skipIf(!capabilities.X) instead of bare if blocks so skipped suites are visible in test output
Typecheck passes
All existing driver test suites (host-dir, overlay, S3, sandbox, SQLite, Postgres) still pass
userStories.notes: Addresses review findings: zero error code assertions, missing createDir test, missing error-path tests, weak stat.size assertion, invisible skipped tests, missing realpath normalization test.

userStories.title: Harden shared test suite: symlink, hardlink, permission, truncate, and pread edge cases
userStories.description: As a driver author, I need the conditional capability tests to cover edge cases found in the adversarial review.
acceptanceCriteria: Add test (symlinks): symlink loop (A -> B -> A) throws ELOOP or EINVAL (gated on symlinks capability)
Add test (symlinks): lstat on symlink returns isSymbolicLink true AND isDirectory false even if target is a directory
Add test (symlinks): realpath on a symlink returns the target's canonical path (gated on symlinks capability)
Add test (symlinks): dangling symlink can be created (symlink to nonexistent target), lstat succeeds, stat/readFile throws ENOENT
Add test (symlinks): removeFile on a symlink removes the symlink itself, not the target file
Add test (symlinks): symlink() on an existing path throws EEXIST
Add test (hardLinks): write to hard link updates content readable from both paths (shared data, not copies)
Add test (hardLinks): link on a directory throws EPERM
Add test (permissions): chmod preserves file type bits (upper bits of mode unchanged, only permission bits change)
Add test (permissions): verify initial mode differs from target mode before chmod assertion
Add test (permissions): verify initial uid/gid differ from target values before chown assertion
Add test (truncate): truncate to zero produces empty file
Add test (truncate): truncate to length longer than file extends with null bytes
Add test (pread): pread with offset and length that are clearly distinguishable (not 3,4 which could be swapped)
Add test (pread): pread beyond file bounds returns available bytes or throws
Add test (utimes): use realistic epoch-ms timestamps (not 1970 values) to catch unit confusion
Typecheck passes
All existing driver test suites still pass
userStories.notes: Addresses review findings: missing symlink loop test, lstat incomplete, removeFile follows symlinks, symlink EEXIST, hardlinks are copies, chmod destroys type bits, weak chmod/chown/pread/truncate tests.

userStories.title: Fix S3 backend: prefix normalization, pagination, pread edge cases, rename encoding
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the S3 backend to handle prefix without trailing slash, paginate large listings, handle pread(length=0), and URL-encode CopySource.
acceptanceCriteria: createS3Backend normalizes prefix to always end with / (or be empty). Passing prefix 'foo' works identically to 'foo/'
readDir and readDirWithTypes paginate using ContinuationToken when IsTruncated is true, returning all entries
pread with length=0 returns empty Uint8Array without sending an invalid Range header
rename URL-encodes the CopySource bucket/key to handle special characters (spaces, #, %, unicode)
rename on missing source throws ENOENT (translate NoSuchKey)
pread handles InvalidRange from S3 gracefully (return available bytes or throw EINVAL)
removeFile on nonexistent file throws ENOENT (check HeadObject before DeleteObject)
Typecheck passes
S3 tests pass
userStories.notes: Addresses review findings: prefix trailing slash corruption, no pagination, pread length=0, CopySource encoding, missing error translations.

userStories.title: Fix sandbox backend: add basePath, fix error handling, mkdir recursive, removeDir semantics
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the sandbox backend to support basePath scoping, stop swallowing all errors as ENOENT, respect mkdir recursive, and not force-delete non-empty dirs.
acceptanceCriteria: SandboxFsOptions includes basePath?: string (default '/'). All operations prefix paths with basePath
readFile catch block only converts not-found SDK errors to ENOENT; network/auth/500 errors propagate as-is
stat catch block only converts not-found SDK errors to ENOENT; network/auth/500 errors propagate as-is
exists catch block returns false only for not-found errors; re-throws network/auth/500
mkdir respects the recursive option: when recursive is false or omitted and parent does not exist, throw ENOENT. Implement recursive by walking up and creating each ancestor
removeDir does NOT pass recursive: true. It removes only empty directories. If the directory is non-empty, throw ENOTEMPTY (check via listFsEntries first)
readDir and readDirWithTypes filter out '.' and '..' entries from SDK results
truncate and pread capabilities set to true in tests (they are implemented and should be tested)
Typecheck passes
Sandbox tests pass
userStories.notes: Addresses review findings: missing basePath, blanket ENOENT conversion, recursive mkdir ignored, removeDir data loss, untested truncate/pread, unfiltered . and .. entries.

userStories.title: Fix SQLite backend: removeFile symlinks, chmod type bits, mkdir recursive, LIKE escaping, rename atomicity
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the SQLite backend to correctly handle removeFile on symlinks, preserve file type bits in chmod, respect mkdir recursive flag, escape LIKE patterns, and make rename fully atomic.
acceptanceCriteria: removeFile does NOT follow symlinks. Calling removeFile on a symlink deletes the symlink entry, not the target
removeFile on a directory throws EISDIR
chmod only modifies permission bits (lower 12 bits). File type bits (upper bits like 0o100000, 0o40000) are preserved
mkdir without recursive: true on a nested path where parent is missing throws ENOENT instead of silently creating parents
readDir/readDirWithTypes/rename child enumeration escape LIKE metacharacters (% and _) in the path prefix
rename is fully atomic: getRow, ensureParentDirs, and all UPDATEs/DELETEs happen inside the same transaction
rename on a destination that is a non-empty directory throws ENOTEMPTY instead of orphaning children
symlink on an existing path throws EEXIST instead of overwriting
resolveSymlinks resolves intermediate path components (e.g. /symlink-to-dir/file.txt follows the symlink in the directory component)
Typecheck passes
SQLite tests pass
userStories.notes: Addresses review findings: removeFile follows symlinks, removeFile can delete dirs, chmod destroys type bits, mkdir always recursive, LIKE injection, rename non-atomic and orphans children, symlink overwrites, intermediate symlinks not resolved.

userStories.title: Fix Postgres backend: SQL injection, removeFile symlinks, chmod type bits, mkdir recursive, LIKE escaping, rename atomicity, truncate/pread via SQL
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Postgres backend to validate schema/prefix identifiers, fix removeFile symlink behavior, preserve chmod type bits, respect mkdir recursive, escape LIKE patterns, make rename atomic, and use SQL substring/overlay for truncate/pread.
acceptanceCriteria: schema and prefix options are validated against /^[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*$/ regex. Invalid values throw an error at construction time
Schema name is properly quoted with double-quote escaping in all SQL statements
removeFile does NOT follow symlinks. Calling removeFile on a symlink deletes the symlink entry, not the target
removeFile on a directory throws EISDIR
chmod only modifies permission bits (lower 12 bits), preserving file type bits
mkdir without recursive: true on a nested path where parent is missing throws ENOENT
readDir/readDirWithTypes/rename child enumeration escape LIKE metacharacters (% and _) in the path prefix
rename is fully atomic: ensureParentDirs, getRow check, destination check, and all UPDATEs/DELETEs happen inside the same transaction
rename on a destination that is a non-empty directory throws ENOTEMPTY
symlink on an existing path throws EEXIST instead of overwriting
link (hard link) is wrapped in a transaction
truncate uses SQL substring on the BYTEA column instead of reading into JS memory
pread uses SQL substring on the BYTEA column instead of reading the entire file into JS memory
resolveSymlinks resolves intermediate path components
writeFile ON CONFLICT also updates ctime_ms
Typecheck passes
Postgres tests pass
userStories.notes: Addresses review findings: SQL injection in schema/prefix, removeFile follows symlinks, chmod destroys type bits, mkdir always recursive, LIKE injection, rename non-atomic, symlink overwrites, link not transactional, truncate/pread don't use SQL, intermediate symlinks not resolved, ctime not updated on overwrite.

userStories.title: Harden Docker test helpers: port validation, crash detection, container cleanup
userStories.description: As a test infrastructure maintainer, I need Docker helpers to fail fast on port resolution errors, detect container crashes, and clean up containers on connect failures.
acceptanceCriteria: startContainer throws a descriptive error if a requested port mapping was not resolved (port value is undefined after docker port)
waitForHealthy detects when a container has exited/crashed (check docker inspect State.Status for 'exited' or 'dead') and fails immediately with a descriptive error instead of polling until timeout
startSandboxAgentContainer wraps SandboxAgent.connect() in try/catch and calls container.stop() if connect fails
startContainer throws a descriptive error if Docker is not installed or daemon is not running (catch ENOENT from execFile and provide a helpful message)
Postgres health check does not interpolate user/database directly into shell command strings (use env vars or array args to avoid injection)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Addresses review findings: silent port resolution failure, blind timeout on crash, container leak on connect failure, unhelpful Docker-not-found error, shell injection in pg health check.
